body.question_1: How would you rate the overall quality of teaching in your courses this semester so far?
body.answer_1: The teaching quality in my Public Relations courses at the School of Communication has been remarkable this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly commend the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_2: How well does the course content align with current industry standards and your career aspirations?
body.answer_2: The curriculum for my Public Relations program at the School of Communication is well-designed and clearly aligned with current industry demands and emerging professional trends in the field. Course structures are logical, building progressively on foundational concepts before introducing advanced topics, which gives students a solid intellectual scaffold. The balance between theoretical foundations and practical application is outstanding, with project-based assessments, case studies, and laboratory work that directly reflect real-world professional scenarios I will encounter in my career. I particularly value the inclusion of contemporary tools and technologies, which ensures graduates are competitive and well-prepared for the demands of the modern job market.

body.question_3: How effective are the assessment methods (assignments, quizzes, exams) in evaluating your understanding of the subject matter?
body.answer_3: Assessment practices in my Public Relations program at the School of Communication are generally moderate, though there is meaningful room for improvement in both consistency and transparency. Some modules employ well-designed assessments that genuinely test comprehension and application, while others rely too heavily on high-stakes end-of-semester examinations that do not adequately reflect students' ongoing learning throughout the term. Timely feedback is provided by some lecturers but is inconsistent across the department. Standardizing minimum feedback turnaround times and encouraging a more diverse portfolio of assessment types would significantly improve the learning experience for all students.

body.question_4: How would you describe the availability and responsiveness of your lecturers outside of scheduled class time?
body.answer_4: Lecturer availability at the School of Communication for my Public Relations courses is adequate, with noticeable differences between individual faculty members in their accessibility and responsiveness to student needs outside of scheduled class sessions. A number of lecturers are readily available and proactive in offering support, which I find invaluable during intensive assignment and examination periods. Others, however, have limited and poorly communicated office hours, making it challenging to get timely guidance on coursework challenges. Establishing a standardized minimum availability requirement and publishing these schedules on the student portal would greatly improve accessibility for all students.

body.question_5: How satisfied are you with the learning resources and materials provided for your courses this semester?
body.answer_5: The learning materials provided for several of my Public Relations courses at the School of Communication are inadequate and fall short of what students need to succeed academically. Required textbooks are outdated and frequently out of stock in both the campus bookstore and the library, forcing students to source materials independently at considerable expense and inconvenience. The Learning Management System is inconsistently used by lecturers — some post materials days after class, and some modules have no digital resources whatsoever. I strongly raise concerns about the Library Services and IT Support Department to collaborate on ensuring all course resources are accessible digitally, current, and available before the semester commences.

body.question_6: How would you rate the IT infrastructure and digital learning tools provided by the university to support your studies?
body.answer_6: The IT infrastructure at the School of Communication, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a reasonable level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_7: How efficient have you found the academic administrative processes such as registration, grade appeals, and scheduling?
body.answer_7: Academic administrative processes at the School of Communication are acceptable in their execution, meeting basic requirements but leaving clear room for improvement in both speed and user-friendliness. The online student portal functions adequately for routine tasks such as registration and fee payment, but navigating more complex requests — such as grade appeals or course exemptions — remains unnecessarily cumbersome. Communication timelines are inconsistent, with some requests handled promptly and others taking weeks without updates or acknowledgment. Streamlining administrative workflows, improving the student portal's UX, and setting clear service-level response standards would meaningfully reduce administrative friction for the entire student body.

body.question_8: How would you evaluate the quality and accessibility of student support services currently available at this university?
body.answer_8: The student support services offered by the Student Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been impressive and have made a genuine difference to my overall well-being and university experience throughout the semester. Counseling services are readily accessible, professionally delivered, and clearly communicated to students through multiple channels including the student portal, orientation sessions, and notice boards. The range of support available — from academic counseling to mental health resources and financial hardship assistance — reflects a comprehensive understanding of the diverse challenges students face during their academic journey. I also value the regular student welfare check-ins organized by the office, which foster a sense of community and belonging on campus.

body.question_9: How well does the university's physical environment and facilities support your daily learning experience?
body.answer_9: Campus facilities at the School of Communication, maintained by the Facilities Management, are generally reasonable and adequate for day-to-day academic activities, though several areas require attention and investment to meet growing needs. Core academic spaces — including lecture halls and laboratories — are functional and reasonably maintained, though some equipment is beginning to show age and inconsistency in performance. Student study and relaxation spaces are insufficient during peak periods, and the condition of older campus buildings varies considerably. A systematic facilities audit followed by a prioritized improvement plan would help address longstanding issues and ensure the campus environment supports the academic ambitions of all students more effectively.

body.question_10: What specific improvements would you most like to see implemented for the remainder of this semester?
body.answer_10: I have very few concerns to raise about my overall experience in the Public Relations program at the School of Communication, and I genuinely believe this institution provides an outstanding educational environment that prepares students well for their future careers. If I were to recommend any improvement, I would suggest further expanding industry partnership opportunities — additional mentorship programs, sponsored capstone projects, and guest lecture series from senior professionals would enrich an already strong academic offering. Increasing the frequency of student feedback collection and demonstrating visible action on that feedback would further strengthen trust between students and the institution. Overall, I am proud to be a student here and would highly recommend this university to future applicants without hesitation.
